titleStmt.title: Letter from Bishop Joseph Hoare to John Francis Hogan, 19 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Joseph Hoare

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Bishop Joseph Hoare (1842-1927) to Rev Dr John Francis Hogan (1858-1918). Joseph Hoare was born in Ballymahon, County Longford and was ordained Bishop of Ardagh and Clonmacnoise on 19 March 1895. He held this post until his death in 1927. John Francis Hogan was born in County Clare and was a priest, author and President of St Patrick's College, Maynooth. Hoare is writing to Hogan to ask for advice and promises that his reply will be treated with confidentiality.

correspAction.placeName: Bishop's House, St Michael's, County Longford, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
2/41 Bishops House.
St Michaels.
Longford. My Dear Monsignor, I
send you an interesting
document. Will you
have the charity
and goodness to
advise me on the
merits of the question. I will
treat your letter
as
confidential.
I am yours sincerely
+ J. Hoare
Dec. 19. 1915
